seoKeywords: how many btu do i need
explanation:
Selecting the right split AC unit requires accurate technical calculations. Proper sizing ensures efficiency, comfort, and energy savings.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator uses the formula: multiply your room size (m²) by the climate factor to get the required BTU. Climate factors are:
- Temperate: 600 BTU/m²
- Warm: 700 BTU/m²
- Very hot: 850 BTU/m²

The calculator verifies your inputs and alerts you if values fall outside typical ranges.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (new installations, large systems, special requirements), consult a licensed HVAC professional. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How many BTU do I need for my room?
faq.a: Multiply your room's square meters by 600–850 (depending on climate). A 20 m² room in a warm climate needs about 14,000 BTU. Use our calculator for precise sizing.

faq.q: What's the difference between BTU and frigorías?
faq.a: 1 BTU/h = 0.252 frigorías. A 3,000-frigoría split unit is approximately 12,000 BTU. Both measure cooling capacity.

faq.q: Should I add extra BTU for a very sunny room?
faq.a: Yes. Rooms with large windows facing the sun need 15–20% additional BTU. South-facing rooms with significant glazing require extra cooling capacity.

faq.q: How much extra BTU for additional people in the room?
faq.a: Add approximately 600 BTU for each person regularly occupying the room beyond standard occupancy.
Yes. Kitchens with major heat sources (stoves, ovens) need about 4,000 additional BTU beyond the base calculation.
It uses internationally validated formulas for standard residential scenarios. Results are approximate. For critical installations or unusual conditions, consult an HVAC professional.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send any data to our servers.
